Documentary following Glasgow businesswoman Celia Sinclair on her mission to rescue the old Willow Tea Rooms on Sauchiehall Street, designed by Charles Rennie Mackintosh.

programmes.show_name: Hercules the Bear - A Love Story
programmes.show_description: The remarkable true story of the world's most famous grizzly bear and the Scottish couple who raised him as their own.
1/3. A cautionary tale: a 16-year-old call centre worker in Glasgow embarks on a five-year crime wave. Contains some strong language.
2/3. On the run: Elliot is stealing and spending across the globe, despite a growing squad of investigators hot on his heels. Contains some strong language.
3/3. Elliot leaves prison in Canada determined to remain at large, but the net is closing in around him. Contains some strong language.

programmes.show_name: The Singer Story: Made in Clydebank
programmes.show_description: Charting how the Singer sewing machine helped establish the town of Clydebank by employing tens of thousands of people before its demise nearly 40 years ago.
Saughton Park regeneration. A neglected site transformed into a model educational facility with a physic garden with the longest flowering border in a public park in the UK.
6/10. A special operations crew in East Ayrshire is at the scene of a two-car collision. Meanwhile, paramedics in Buckie meet a man who crafts handmade fishing nets.
3/6. Sitcom. Jack and Victor gather a team for the walking football tournament. They absolutely must win, but will they be match-fit or have to employ other tactics? Contains adult humour.
5/5. A choice selection of arresting moments, featuring classic cases and favourite files.

Gary Robertson brings you all the main issues from the world of politics and beyond. Big interviews, discussion and analysis of the stories that matter to Scotland.
